title: Phone Not Powering On
description: Use this when a phone seems dead, does not start, stays black after the power key, is stuck on the Samsung logo, or only vibrates.
immediate_action: Confirm what the customer sees right now, then ask the guided yes or no questions before promising repair intake.
explanation: This flow separates charger problems, deep battery drain, display faults, forced-restart recoveries, and true internal power failures.
warranty_status: Depends on the final diagnosis and any visible damage.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'I'll help you check the phone step by step so we can choose the safest next action.'
customer_listening: Let the customer explain what happened before the phone stopped powering on and whether it recently shut down, got wet, or overheated.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'This branch check takes a few quick questions, then I'll explain whether we can recover it here or whether it really needs repair.'
related_actions: Check the screen issue flow if the phone vibrates but the display stays dark.|Offer repair booking only when the branch check points to internal power failure.

title: Screen Issue
description: Use this for cracked displays, blacked-out screens, lines, shaky pictures, blurry output, yellow tint, ghost touch, and touch problems.
immediate_action: Check the screen condition with the customer in front of you before you promise a fix or quote.
explanation: This flow separates visible damage, touch failure, temporary software behavior, display-setting changes, and internal display faults.
warranty_status: Manufacturing faults may be covered. Cracks and impact damage are usually not covered.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'Let's look at the screen together and I'll explain each next step in simple terms.'
customer_listening: Ask when the display problem started and whether the phone was dropped
column14: Set expectation: 'I'll check whether this looks like visible damage, a temporary issue, or something that needs repair.'
column15: Link back to the power flow if the phone will not show any life at all.|Capture photos of visible cracks before handover.

title: Stolen Phone
description: Use this when a customer says the phone was stolen, snatched, lost, or is no longer in their possession.
immediate_action: Slow the conversation down, confirm ownership details, and move straight into device protection steps.
explanation: This flow helps the branch officer decide whether to focus on tracking, account protection, ownership proof, or reporting.
warranty_status: Theft is not covered under standard device warranty.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'I'm sorry this happened. Let's secure the account and device details first.'
customer_listening: Let the customer explain when the phone was taken and what access they still have.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'We'll focus on protection steps first, then I'll show you what can still be done from the branch side.'
related_actions: Offer SIM block guidance if the number is still active.|Advise the customer to change account passwords immediately if they still have access.

title: Charging Issue
description: Use this when the phone does not charge, charges slowly, only charges in one position, shows moisture or port warnings, or loses battery while plugged in.
immediate_action: Check the cable, charger, charging port history, and visible alerts before you assume the battery is faulty.
explanation: This flow helps the branch officer separate accessory faults, port-side problems, usage behavior, battery wear, and deeper charging issues.
warranty_status: Depends on physical condition and the final charging diagnosis.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'We'll check the charging basics first so we don't send the phone for repair too early.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the issue is slow charging, no charging, one-position charging, moisture warning, or battery draining too fast while plugged in.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'I'll help you rule out the common causes first, then I'll explain whether this needs repair.'
related_actions: Move to the power flow if the phone stops showing any life at all.|Check screen flow if the customer reports vibration without visible display during charging.

title: Overheating or Swollen Battery
description: Use this when the phone gets too hot, shows a temperature warning, smells burnt, or has a swollen battery or lifting back cover.
immediate_action: Treat safety first. Ask whether the phone is hot right now, charging, swollen, or giving off a smell before any other step.
explanation: This flow separates unsafe battery swelling, heavy-use heat, charger-related heat, and persistent thermal faults.
warranty_status: Swollen or burnt battery cases need inspection before any warranty promise is made.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'Let's make sure the phone is safe to handle first, then we'll decide the next step.'
customer_listening: Ask when the heat started and whether the phone was charging, in the sun, gaming, or using hotspot.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'If this looks unsafe, I will stop the quick checks and guide you to the safest repair step.'
related_actions: Move to the charging issue flow if the heat appears only while using one charger.|Move to the battery-drain flow if the main complaint is fast discharge without major heat.

title: Battery Draining Fast
description: Use this when the battery finishes quickly, drops overnight, or the charge percentage falls too fast during light use.
immediate_action: Ask how fast the battery drops, whether this started recently, and whether the phone is also heating or shutting down.
explanation: This flow separates app or settings drain, repeat battery-health complaints, and power faults that need inspection.
warranty_status: Depends on battery health, visible damage, and the final inspection result.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'Let's check whether this is something the branch can correct before the phone is sent away.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the drain started after an app, update, hotspot use, or another recent change.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'I'll first rule out the common battery-drain causes that can be fixed at branch level.'
related_actions: Move to the overheating flow if the phone is also getting hot or swelling.|Move to the charging flow if the battery never reaches a full charge.

title: Freezing, Hanging, or App Issue
description: Use this when the phone freezes, hangs, becomes very slow, delays when opening apps, or a customer reports app crashes and stuck screens.
immediate_action: Ask whether the problem happens in one app or across the whole phone before you assume the device needs repair.
explanation: This flow helps branch officers separate app-specific faults, storage pressure, pending updates, temporary lag, and broader software instability.
warranty_status: Software-led issues are not a warranty decision until hardware causes are ruled out.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'We'll first check if this is a software or app problem that can be solved here.'
customer_listening: Ask when the freezing started and whether it is linked to one app, a recent update, low storage, or the phone feeling hot.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'If the easy software checks fix it, we can avoid sending the phone away unnecessarily.'
related_actions: Move to the random restart or safe mode flow if the phone is rebooting on its own.|Move to the screen flow only if the display itself is damaged or distorted.

title: Random Restart or Safe Mode Issue
description: Use this when the phone restarts by itself, keeps rebooting, or gets stuck in safe mode.
immediate_action: Confirm whether the phone is in safe mode now or if the restarts are random before assuming a hardware defect.
explanation: This flow separates stuck-button or safe-mode behavior, app-triggered restarts, and repeat restart faults that need escalation.
warranty_status: No warranty decision until the restart pattern and physical condition are confirmed.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'Let's check whether the restart behavior can be solved here before we book a repair.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the restart began after an update, one app, a new case, or a button problem.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'If a simple branch check clears the safe mode or restart trigger, we can save time and courier cost.'
related_actions: Move to the freezing flow if the phone mostly hangs but does not reboot.|Move to the power flow if the phone now shows no life at all.

title: SIM or Network Issue
description: Use this when the phone does not read the SIM, shows no service, loses mobile data, fails to register to network, or cannot hold network reliably.
immediate_action: Separate SIM detection and mobile-signal complaints from Wi-Fi-only complaints before you assume the device needs board repair.
explanation: This flow helps branch staff rule out SIM-side problems, coverage issues, APN or airplane-mode mistakes, and basic phone-side resets before escalation.
warranty_status: No final warranty decision until SIM, coverage, and damage causes are ruled out.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'Let's first rule out the simple SIM and network causes that can be handled here.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the problem is no SIM, no signal, no mobile data, delayed network registration, or Wi-Fi only.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'If the signal comes back after the branch checks, we can avoid an unnecessary service-center trip.'
related_actions: Move to the audio issue flow if the complaint is mainly about call hearing rather than signal.|Move to the physical damage flow if the SIM tray or frame is visibly damaged.

title: Speaker, Microphone, or Audio Issue
description: Use this when the customer cannot hear, cannot be heard, has low speaker output, or reports mouthpiece, earpiece, or microphone trouble.
immediate_action: Rule out mute, Bluetooth, headset mode, and low volume before booking audio repair.
explanation: This flow helps branch staff separate simple audio-routing problems from repeat component faults.
warranty_status: No final warranty decision until settings, signal, and visible damage causes are ruled out.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'Let's first check the easy audio settings that often solve this without sending the phone away.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the customer cannot hear, cannot be heard, or both, and whether it happens in calls, media, or recordings.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'If the sound comes back after the branch checks, we can avoid an unnecessary repair trip.'
related_actions: Move to the network issue flow if the complaint is only about call quality and poor signal.|Move to the physical damage flow if the frame, grill, or speaker area is visibly damaged.

title: FRP, Password, or Locked Device
description: Use this when the customer forgot a password or pattern, faces FRP or Google lock, or brings a managed or restricted device.
immediate_action: Confirm ownership before discussing account recovery or reset guidance.
explanation: This flow separates ownership proof, FRP or Google lock, forgotten credentials, and managed-device restrictions.
warranty_status: Credential loss and FRP are not standard hardware warranty issues.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'I'll first confirm the ownership side, then I'll explain the safest recovery path.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the phone belongs to the customer, what lock screen is showing, and whether the issue started after a reset.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'This is handled as an access and ownership process, not a normal repair diagnosis.'
related_actions: Move to the stolen phone flow if ownership is uncertain and the device may not belong to the customer.|Do not promise bypass actions for security locks.

title: Liquid or Physical Damage
description: Use this when the phone fell in water, was dropped, is bent, burnt, has a broken SIM tray, or shows obvious external damage.
immediate_action: Check for visible impact, liquid, corrosion, bend, or burn signs before you book any standard repair path.
explanation: This flow helps branch staff document external damage correctly and avoid misclassifying damage as a normal in-warranty fault.
warranty_status: Physical or liquid damage is usually outside standard warranty unless inspection policy says otherwise.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'Let's first confirm whether there is visible damage or liquid contact, because that changes the repair path.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the phone was dropped, got wet, bent, burnt, or broke at one external part like the SIM tray.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'If this is physical or liquid damage, I will explain the correct repair route and warranty position clearly.'
related_actions: Move to the screen issue flow if the main complaint is display behavior after visible damage.|Move to the charging or overheating flow if the port or battery behavior changed after the damage event.

title: Repair Ticket, Dispatch, or Legal Status Handling
description: Use this when the branch must classify a repair, log movement, prepare dispatch, or decide which legal status request and destination should be used.
immediate_action: Classify the case correctly before dispatch. The wrong warranty path or legal status request creates cost, SLA, and traceability problems.
explanation: This workflow helps the branch separate in-warranty handling, out-warranty handling, forgotten-password processing, and uncertain cases that need clarification before dispatch.
warranty_status: In-warranty faults are free. Physical damage, tampering, and forgotten passwords are out-warranty.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'I'll first classify the case correctly so the phone goes through the right repair and tracking path.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the problem is manufacturer-originating, physically damaged, tampered with, or a forgotten-password case.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'The classification decides the cost, legal status request, and whether the phone goes to Transtel or Watu SIMU HQ.'
related_actions: Every movement must be logged in the branch-specific Aftersales Ticketing System and Aftersales Sheet.|All signed forms, job cards, return forms, and waybills must be scanned and uploaded immediately.|Repair and return devices must have the sticker section attached before dispatch.

title: Replacement Request Eligibility
description: Use this when a customer asks for a replacement device after theft or BER and the branch must confirm eligibility before moving the case forward.
immediate_action: Confirm the payment count, theft or BER status, and prior replacement history before promising a replacement device.
explanation: This workflow helps the branch separate eligible replacement cases from cases that still need legal status, payment, or return handling first.
warranty_status: Replacement is not automatic. It depends on payments, verified status, and prior replacement history.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'I'll confirm the replacement conditions first so I don't promise a device before the account is eligible.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the case is theft or BER, how many weekly payments have been made, and whether the device already has the correct legal status.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'Replacement depends on both payment history and the correct legal status, so I'll check those first.'
related_actions: Replacement processing runs through OBS, similar to a new sale.|Replacement devices may not include accessories.|Before handover, confirm the relock timestamp is 7 days from loan start and verify Watu App access.

title: Asset Transfer or Loan Reschedule
description: Use this when a customer asks to transfer device ownership, transfer after death, or reschedule a loan because a faulty device has stayed too long at the repair centre.
immediate_action: Check arrears, presence of both parties, legal documents, and repair-centre timing before promising a transfer or reschedule.
explanation: This workflow separates normal transfer requests, death-related transfer or return decisions, and long-delay repair-centre reschedule cases.
warranty_status: Transfers and reschedules follow operational eligibility rules, not normal repair warranty rules.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'I'll first check whether this is a transfer case or a long-delay repair case, because the branch rules are different.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the customer wants a transfer, a next-of-kin takeover, or relief because the faulty device has stayed too long in repair.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'The branch can only move forward after the eligibility checks are complete, so I'll verify those first.'
related_actions: Transfers are created in OBS with Loan Type 'Transferred'.|Transfers are blocked when the loan is overdue or almost cleared.|Reschedule requests need proof from the repair centre and timing beyond 15 days without Watu Repairs status.

title: Return, Refund, or Recovered Device Handling
description: Use this when a customer wants to return a device for refund, a recovered device is brought back, or the branch must decide whether recovery, refund, or storage rules apply.
immediate_action: Confirm who is returning the device, the device condition, and whether the case is a normal refund, a recovered device, or a third-party return.
explanation: This workflow separates owner-led refund requests, non-refundable return scenarios, and recovered-device handling with rewards or storage rules.
warranty_status: Refunds and recoveries follow ownership, condition, and traceability rules.
customer_greeting: Start with: 'I'll first confirm whether this is a refund return, a recovered device, or a third-party handover so the branch follows the right path.'
customer_listening: Ask whether the owner is present, whether the device is damaged, and whether the phone is a recovered device being handed in.
customer_expectation: Set expectation: 'The refund and recovery rules are different, so I'll confirm the exact case before I advise the next step.'
related_actions: Collect the phone password and perform factory reset for return processing.|Check the IMEI or loan account against the Counter Flash list and the Reported Thefts list.|Upload all signed documents and keep the physical traceability chain complete.
